allowed_workspace_edges.rationale: debug consumes HIR symbols
debug/runtime split tracked by architecture program
trust-dev is the workbench CLI package and consumes the runtime public API plus compatibility command surfaces
trust-dev documentation and test helpers consume syntax parsing APIs
trust-dev keeps existing docs/workbench analysis behavior during package extraction
HIR lowers syntax
IDE consumes HIR analysis
IDE consumes syntax facts
LSP consumes analysis
LSP delegates IDE services
runtime-backed debug/protocol helpers tracked by boundary policy
LSP syntax integration
runtime harness/compiler consumes HIR
web IDE hosted from runtime until product/workbench split
runtime exposes PLCopen compatibility reexports from the extracted trust-plcopen package
Linux host consumes portable runtime core through compatibility bridge
optional hir feature preserves host-side value construction APIs; no-default core check proves pure core does not require HIR
PLCopen import/export parses Structured Text fragments through syntax APIs
runtime compiler consumes syntax
browser analysis surface tracked by runtime surface split
WASM analysis consumes HIR
WASM IDE integration tracked by boundary policy
WASM analysis consumes syntax

runtime_command_classes.name: Test
runtime_command_classes.rationale: dev test helper command to split

runtime_bin_module_classes.name: dev_forward
runtime_bin_module_classes.rationale: deprecated trust-runtime workbench aliases forward to trust-dev

runtime_bin_module_classes.name: git
runtime_bin_module_classes.rationale: git helper used by setup and workbench commands; split decision tracked by runtime CLI board

runtime_bin_module_classes.name: prompt
runtime_bin_module_classes.rationale: interactive prompt helper shared by setup and workbench commands

runtime_bin_module_classes.name: style
runtime_bin_module_classes.rationale: terminal formatting helper shared by product and workbench commands

runtime_bin_module_classes.name: ci
runtime_bin_module_classes.rationale: stable --ci exit-code mapping used by product build/validate aliases and workbench commands

runtime_command_module_routes.rationale: Command::Play dispatches to run::run_play from the shared runtime launcher module
Command::Ui dispatches to trust_runtime::ui::run_ui from the runtime UI library surface
Command::Validate dispatches to run::run_validate from the shared runtime launcher module
Command::Ide dispatches to config_ui::run_ide_serve and shares ConfigUiAction with Command::ConfigUi
Command::Rollback dispatches to deploy::run_rollback from the deployment module

runtime_artifact_profiles.name: release-host-runtime
runtime_artifact_profiles.rationale: current release bundle preserves the existing trust-runtime command surface and ships trust-dev as the workbench destination during the forwarding-alias migration window

runtime_artifact_profiles.name: field-runtime-minimal
runtime_artifact_profiles.rationale: field runtime artifacts must exclude workbench/dev command implementations; benchmark/conformance commands are runtime-adjacent but not field-minimal

runtime_workbench_command_migrations.rationale: agent serve remains available through trust-runtime during the deprecation window while the implementation moves to trust-dev
project git helper belongs to the developer/workbench tool and keeps a trust-runtime compatibility alias during migration
ST API documentation generation belongs to the developer/workbench tool and keeps a trust-runtime compatibility alias during migration
ST test discovery/execution belongs to the developer/workbench tool and keeps a trust-runtime compatibility alias during migration

owned_paths.rationale: control module root and ControlState live in the control boundary
control handlers and policy own HTTP-neutral command/query ports
HMI module root owns schema and descriptor exports
HMI contracts, descriptors, scaffold, and view projection remain domain-owned
web module root owns HTTP server wiring and static asset routing
web subtree owns HTTP routes, websocket transport, browser assets, and adapters
terminal/local UI module root owns presentation entrypoints
terminal/local UI subtree owns presentation state and rendering
runtime-cloud contracts, projection, routing, keyspace, and HA policy remain transport-neutral

forbidden_edges.rationale: control must not depend on web implementation
HMI domain must not depend on web transport
runtime-cloud domain must not depend on web transport implementation

runtime_top_level_module_decisions.name: host
runtime_top_level_module_decisions.rationale: collapsed physical namespace for host-only adapters, dev/build support, protocol surfaces, observability, registry, setup, and compatibility shims; public module paths remain declared in lib.rs through explicit path attributes

runtime_top_level_module_decisions.name: io
runtime_top_level_module_decisions.rationale: host IO drivers and IO configuration

runtime_top_level_module_decisions.name: web
runtime_top_level_module_decisions.rationale: HTTP routes, websocket transport, and browser assets

module_size_allowlist.rationale: runtime execution remains the largest host orchestration module after core extraction
module_size_allowlist.split_plan: Continue extracting VM execution internals into register_ir/vm child modules and avoid adding new top-level runtime execution files.

module_size_allowlist.rationale: LSP request handlers are intentionally grouped by protocol surface
module_size_allowlist.split_plan: Split only when a handler family grows a separate protocol owner or introduces cross-handler state.

module_size_allowlist.rationale: binary host modules retain compatibility wrappers while workbench commands migrate to trust-dev
module_size_allowlist.split_plan: Remove deprecated trust-runtime workbench aliases after the compatibility window, then remeasure the binary host module.

module_size_allowlist.rationale: host-only adapters, protocol integrations, build/dev helpers, registry, setup, debug, observability, and compatibility shims are physically grouped to keep the runtime host top-level shape below the final cap
module_size_allowlist.split_plan: Keep child ownership under host/*; extract a child crate only when a host family gains an independently versioned API or dependency boundary.

module_size_allowlist.rationale: web remains the HTTP adapter surface for IDE, HMI, config, runtime-cloud, and auxiliary routes
module_size_allowlist.split_plan: Extract route families only behind existing control/HMI/runtime-cloud ports; do not move domain state into web.

module_size_allowlist.rationale: HIR Salsa database/query ownership is intentionally centralized
module_size_allowlist.split_plan: Split generated/query-family helpers only when the HIR zero-silent-bug doctor can preserve the semantic-kernel boundary.

module_size_allowlist.rationale: type checking is a high-density semantic area covered by HIR zero-silent-bug gates
module_size_allowlist.split_plan: Prefer semantic-kernel helpers over broad module moves; split only with mutation-backed semantic tests.

module_size_allowlist.rationale: debug adapter protocol support has protocol-local size but limited runtime ownership risk
module_size_allowlist.split_plan: Split by protocol state/request families when debug adapter changes resume.

module_size_allowlist.rationale: PLCopen XML import/export is isolated from the Linux runtime host but remains a large subsystem with parser/model/export child files
module_size_allowlist.split_plan: Split import/export/parser/test families inside trust-plcopen when PLCopen interchange work resumes.

module_size_allowlist.rationale: HMI domain contracts and projections are grouped behind the HMI port boundary
module_size_allowlist.split_plan: Keep future splits inside HMI descriptor/trend/alarm/layout domains; web remains only a transport adapter.

module_size_allowlist.rationale: control command/query API owns the host surface port used by web, HMI, and debug surfaces
module_size_allowlist.split_plan: Continue moving handler families into child modules while preserving the single control port entrypoint.

module_size_allowlist.rationale: host bytecode encoder/compatibility support remains in trust-runtime while portable bytecode pieces live in trust-runtime-core
module_size_allowlist.split_plan: Move only host-agnostic bytecode pieces to trust-runtime-core when public API compatibility allows it.

module_size_allowlist.rationale: full-map doctor owns the architecture-program policy checks and generated report logic
module_size_allowlist.split_plan: Split policy models, source scanners, and check evaluators into child modules if full-map doctor work continues after program closeout.

function_size_allowlist.name: handle_workspace_routes
function_size_allowlist.rationale: workspace route aggregator handles several HTTP verbs in one route family
function_size_allowlist.split_plan: Split GET/POST workspace actions into child handlers if this route changes again.

function_size_allowlist.name: execute_register_block_interpreted
function_size_allowlist.rationale: register-IR interpreted execution loop is mutation-locked and kept as one dispatch path
function_size_allowlist.split_plan: Extract instruction-family helpers only with runtime VM mutation shard coverage.

function_size_allowlist.name: run_runtime
function_size_allowlist.rationale: runtime command launch orchestration is behavior-sensitive and already split into settings/control helpers
function_size_allowlist.split_plan: Extract remaining launch phases after adding behavior-lock tests for CLI startup output.

function_size_allowlist.name: handle_ui_route
function_size_allowlist.rationale: UI route dispatch remains a transport adapter over static assets and control projections
function_size_allowlist.split_plan: Split static asset and action handling if web UI routing grows again.

function_size_allowlist.name: handle_language_route
function_size_allowlist.rationale: IDE language route aggregates analysis query cases behind the web adapter
function_size_allowlist.split_plan: Split request cases into typed child handlers if the IDE HTTP API changes.

function_size_allowlist.name: execute_tier1_compiled_block
function_size_allowlist.rationale: tier1 compiled execution is mutation-locked and kept as a single hot loop
function_size_allowlist.split_plan: Extract instruction-family helpers only under the tier1 mutation shards.

function_size_allowlist.name: execute_pou_stack_with_locals
function_size_allowlist.rationale: VM stack/local execution remains a core behavior-lock target
function_size_allowlist.split_plan: Split stack frame setup from instruction execution only with complete_program and VM mutation coverage.

function_size_allowlist.name: handle_config_set
function_size_allowlist.rationale: config set handler centralizes validation and writeback policy
function_size_allowlist.split_plan: Split per-section setters behind the same control request contract.

function_size_allowlist.name: handle_basic_route
function_size_allowlist.rationale: basic IDE route handler is a web transport adapter for several simple endpoints
function_size_allowlist.split_plan: Split only when endpoint ownership diverges.

function_size_allowlist.name: handle_file_route
function_size_allowlist.rationale: file route handler keeps workspace file operations in one HTTP family
function_size_allowlist.split_plan: Split read/write/list operations if IDE file API changes.

function_size_allowlist.name: build_runtime_from_source_files
function_size_allowlist.rationale: harness build orchestration coordinates parser, HIR, runtime, and diagnostics for tests
function_size_allowlist.split_plan: Extract compile phases only with harness regression coverage.

function_size_allowlist.name: lower_pou_to_register_ir
function_size_allowlist.rationale: register-IR lowering root is mutation-locked after BOARD-10
function_size_allowlist.split_plan: Move more decode/fuse/verify phases to existing child modules when lowering changes.

function_size_allowlist.name: handle_post_dispatch
function_size_allowlist.rationale: runtime-cloud dispatch route owns one request/response transaction shape
function_size_allowlist.split_plan: Split action decoding and response projection if runtime-cloud action contracts grow.

function_size_allowlist.name: encode_type_entry
function_size_allowlist.rationale: type encoding is compatibility-sensitive and tied to bytecode fixtures
function_size_allowlist.split_plan: Extract per-type encoders only with bytecode encode/decode parity tests.

function_size_allowlist.name: invalid_and_malformed_requests_return_negative_responses
function_size_allowlist.rationale: negative response matrix intentionally keeps related malformed request cases together
function_size_allowlist.split_plan: Split control negative tests by request family if test maintenance suffers.

function_size_allowlist.name: handle_ops_route
function_size_allowlist.rationale: ops route dispatch is a web transport adapter over control operations
function_size_allowlist.split_plan: Split operation families only behind existing control contracts.

function_size_allowlist.name: handle_runtime_cloud_routes
function_size_allowlist.rationale: config UI runtime-cloud route keeps related HTTP actions in one route family
function_size_allowlist.split_plan: Split by runtime-cloud action category if this route grows.

function_size_allowlist.name: execute_register_subset
function_size_allowlist.rationale: test support helper covers a broad register-IR setup/execution path
function_size_allowlist.split_plan: Split setup from execution helpers when register-IR tests are reorganized.

function_size_allowlist.name: collect_io_bindings
function_size_allowlist.rationale: I/O binding collection owns one harness lowering pass
function_size_allowlist.split_plan: Split address parsing from binding projection if I/O harness logic grows.

function_size_allowlist.name: try_fuse_instruction_window
function_size_allowlist.rationale: fusion window logic is mutation-sensitive and kept in one helper
function_size_allowlist.split_plan: Split fusion cases only under the register-ir-lower-fuse mutation shard.

function_size_allowlist.name: emit_expr
function_size_allowlist.rationale: expression bytecode emission mirrors expression semantics and is parity-sensitive
function_size_allowlist.split_plan: Split expression families only with bytecode/runtime parity tests.

function_size_allowlist.name: apply_globals
function_size_allowlist.rationale: global application is one harness configuration pass
function_size_allowlist.split_plan: Split type/default/write phases if harness config grows.

function_size_allowlist.name: scaffold_hmi_dir_with_sources_mode
function_size_allowlist.rationale: HMI scaffold entrypoint coordinates source discovery and generated descriptor output
function_size_allowlist.split_plan: Split source collection from descriptor writing if scaffold work resumes.

function_size_allowlist.name: exec_stmt
function_size_allowlist.rationale: statement evaluator remains a compatibility path for runtime semantics
function_size_allowlist.split_plan: Extract statement-family helpers only with evaluator/VM parity tests.

function_size_allowlist.name: handle_post_control_proxy
function_size_allowlist.rationale: control proxy route owns a single runtime-cloud transport-to-control bridge
function_size_allowlist.split_plan: Split request translation from response projection if proxy contracts grow.

function_size_allowlist.name: render_table
function_size_allowlist.rationale: benchmark table rendering is isolated presentation logic
function_size_allowlist.split_plan: Split formatting helpers if benchmark report output grows.

function_size_allowlist.name: runtime_status_projection_contract_reports_resource_metrics_realtime_and_io_health
function_size_allowlist.rationale: status projection contract intentionally covers a broad status payload
function_size_allowlist.split_plan: Split by status section only if the control status contract changes.

function_size_allowlist.name: start_web_server_with_mode
function_size_allowlist.rationale: server startup coordinates TLS, route mode, and request loop setup
function_size_allowlist.split_plan: Extract TLS/server construction from request loop when web server internals change.

function_size_allowlist.name: menu_entries
function_size_allowlist.rationale: menu entry table is large data construction rather than hidden control flow
function_size_allowlist.split_plan: Move static menu definitions to data tables if terminal UI navigation grows.

public_api_snapshots.rationale: runtime host public API growth must be reviewed against a tracked baseline
portable runtime-core public API growth must be reviewed against a tracked baseline

dependency_hygiene_tools.name: cargo audit
dependency_hygiene_tools.rationale: raw advisories are fixed or explicitly ignored by policy metadata

dependency_hygiene_tools.name: cargo machete
dependency_hygiene_tools.rationale: workspace member paths have no unused direct dependencies

dependency_hygiene_tools.name: cargo deny
dependency_hygiene_tools.rationale: project deny.toml policy is present and cargo deny check passes

third_party_tiverse_mmap.rationale: vendored tiverse-mmap is a crates.io patch source and must not be an implicit workspace member

audit_allowlist.rationale: optional opcua 0.12 pulls url 1.7/idna 0.1 and no newer opcua crate is available
audit_allowlist.removal_condition: remove when opcua upgrades url/idna or the opcua-wire feature is replaced

audit_allowlist.rationale: optional opcua 0.12 pulls derivative 2.2.0 and no newer opcua crate is available
audit_allowlist.removal_condition: remove when opcua removes derivative or the opcua-wire feature is replaced

audit_allowlist.rationale: tiny_http 0.12 ssl-rustls pulls rustls 0.20/ring 0.16 for the existing HTTPS server and no tiny_http rustls 0.23 release is available
audit_allowlist.removal_condition: remove when HTTPS hosting moves off tiny_http ssl-rustls or tiny_http upgrades rustls

audit_allowlist.rationale: zenoh 1.7.2 transport stack pulls rsa 0.9 and the pinned baseline is governed by the runtime mesh compatibility gate
audit_allowlist.removal_condition: remove when the Zenoh baseline gate moves to a patched transport stack

audit_allowlist.rationale: tiny_http 0.12 ssl-rustls pulls rustls 0.20 for the existing HTTPS server and no tiny_http rustls 0.23 release is available
audit_allowlist.removal_condition: remove when HTTPS hosting moves off tiny_http ssl-rustls or tiny_http upgrades rustls

audit_allowlist.rationale: tiny_http 0.12 ssl-rustls pulls unmaintained ring 0.16 for the existing HTTPS server and no tiny_http rustls 0.23 release is available
audit_allowlist.removal_condition: remove when HTTPS hosting moves off tiny_http ssl-rustls or tiny_http upgrades rustls

audit_allowlist.rationale: rustls-pemfile is used by current TLS PEM parsing and tiny_http 0.12 ssl-rustls
audit_allowlist.removal_condition: replace direct parsing with rustls-pki-types PemObject and remove tiny_http ssl-rustls

audit_allowlist.rationale: paste is transitive through ratatui and zenoh token-cell
audit_allowlist.removal_condition: remove when ratatui/zenoh transitive paths stop pulling paste

unsafe_site_register.invariant: test-only allocation counter implements GlobalAlloc by forwarding every allocation operation to System with the same pointer/layout contract
test-only allocation counter forwards allocation layout unchanged to System
System.alloc receives the same Layout supplied by the global allocator hook
test-only allocation counter forwards deallocation pointer/layout unchanged to System
System.dealloc receives the original pointer/layout pair supplied by the allocator hook
test-only allocation counter forwards reallocation pointer/layout/new-size unchanged to System
System.realloc receives the original pointer/layout and requested new size supplied by the allocator hook

delegated_unsafe_path_register.invariant: vendored mmap unsafe remains isolated behind typed mapping/file-lock abstractions and is excluded from the workspace member set
vendored examples are demonstration code only and remain outside shipped runtime/library execution paths

panic_like_classifications.rationale: runtime web panic-like sites are static header construction, response serialization fallbacks, or request-bound transport glue; they are tracked separately from VM/scheduler execution hot paths
control handlers serialize project-owned response structs; remaining expect sites are tracked as serialization invariants with HMI/control tests
debug-control panic-like sites are request/response serialization or lock-state access points covered by debug control integration tests
scheduler poison panics were removed in BOARD-11; remaining panic-like sites are saturating conversions or explicit non-panicking fallbacks

panic_like_classifications.classification: VM internal invariant or cfg-test helper
panic_like_classifications.rationale: VM execution-buffer expect sites guard impossible post-acquire states and cfg-test helpers are excluded from production scans
production helper-eval sites are tracked and inline cfg-test panic sites are excluded from production scans
harness panics are explicit developer/test guardrails rather than product runtime cycle execution
memory cache poison panics were removed in BOARD-11; remaining panic-like sites are tracked storage invariants
realtime panic-like sites are test/support routes or checked conversion fallbacks outside normal VM cycle execution
runtime-cloud panic-like sites are projection/config serialization and policy checks, not shared VM core execution
I/O adapter panic-like sites are startup/test adapter invariants and remain outside core VM execution
binary panic-like sites are CLI/workbench startup validation or developer tooling guards
catch-all for remaining reviewed runtime panic-like sites after scheduler/memory hot-path poison panics were removed; new unreviewed files outside runtime src still fail
HIR panic-like sites are tracked by semantic zero-silent-bug and query tests; they are not runtime hot-path execution panics
LSP panic-like sites are server state/test-support/performance harness invariants and remain outside runtime cycle execution
IDE panic-like sites are analysis fallback or test-support invariants and remain outside runtime cycle execution

concurrency_boundaries.shared_state: resource state, restart signals, command channels, manual clocks
concurrency_boundaries.invariant: state transitions are serialized and poison is recovered before updating resource status/error

concurrency_boundaries.shared_state: control server state and request/response channels
concurrency_boundaries.invariant: control requests use owned messages and synchronize through explicit handler state

concurrency_boundaries.shared_state: web server state, websocket clients, HMI/control transport
concurrency_boundaries.invariant: web routes are transport adapters and do not mutate runtime state outside approved ports
runtime-cloud state is owned by runtime-cloud modules and exposed through policy/projection APIs

concurrency_boundaries.shared_state: I/O adapter sessions and protocol clients
concurrency_boundaries.invariant: I/O adapters own external protocol state and communicate with runtime through configured driver boundaries

concurrency_boundaries.shared_state: mesh startup publication and runtime identity snapshots
concurrency_boundaries.invariant: mesh snapshots are copied out of runtime state before publication
security state is isolated behind pairing/session APIs

concurrency_boundaries.shared_state: T0 transport buffers and Linux realtime setup
concurrency_boundaries.invariant: realtime transport keeps host-specific synchronization outside trust-runtime-core

concurrency_boundaries.shared_state: runtime host state, protocol adapters, debug/control surfaces, and support services
concurrency_boundaries.invariant: runtime concurrency remains behind owned subsystem APIs; scheduler/memory poison panics were removed in BOARD-11 and subsystem-specific boundaries above own the highest-risk paths
concurrency_boundaries.test_evidence: cargo test -p trust-runtime --test api_smoke --test debug_control --test runtime_reliability

concurrency_boundaries.invariant: trust-runtime-core remains host-agnostic and does not own OS threads or host synchronization primitives
Salsa-owned concurrency remains behind HIR query APIs

concurrency_boundaries.shared_state: HIR semantic data and query-local state
concurrency_boundaries.invariant: HIR shared state remains behind semantic/query APIs and is covered by HIR zero-silent-bug gates

concurrency_boundaries.shared_state: LSP server state, telemetry, workspace indexing, and request tasks
concurrency_boundaries.invariant: LSP async/concurrent state remains behind ServerState, telemetry, and handler boundaries

concurrency_boundaries.shared_state: IDE analysis caches and utility state
concurrency_boundaries.invariant: IDE state remains request-local or behind owned analysis APIs
server state is synchronized through the LSP state module and request handlers
allocation counters are atomic and only active in the perf_alloc_metrics test feature

tool_gates.name: cargo geiger advisory
tool_gates.blocker: cargo-geiger 0.13.0 rejects the workspace virtual manifest; absolute package-manifest probe starts by cleaning/rebuilding a large target tree, so repo-specific full-map unsafe scanning is the enforced first-party gate and geiger remains advisory

tool_gates.name: Loom/model equivalent
tool_gates.evidence: 3 deterministic poison-recovery tests passed for scheduler clock, scheduler runner loop, and memory cache clone
